user_query:
I need you to consolidate our mixed-format project notes into five organized documentation trackers. Read all the files under input/ and write the trackers under output/project/ with the exact filenames below. Rules and schema are strict so we can automate downstream checks.

Inputs to read:
- input/features.jsonl
- input/code_style.yaml
- input/security_issues.csv
- input/ui_styles.json
- input/todos.tsv

Create these output files with strict JSON schemas:
1) output/project/feature-tracker.json
   - Schema (top-level object):
     - category: "features"
     - source_files: ["input/features.jsonl"]
     - counts: { total: <int> }
     - entries: array of objects with keys [id, title, status, description, component]
   - Transformations:
     - Read JSON Lines from input/features.jsonl.
     - Deduplicate by id, keeping the last occurrence in the file.
     - Coerce id to string.
     - Normalize status to lowercase.
     - Sort entries by id ascending (lexicographic on the stringified id).

2) output/project/code-style-tracker.json
   - Schema:
     - category: "code_style"
     - source_files: ["input/code_style.yaml"]
     - counts: { total: <int> }
     - entries: array with keys [id, rule, rationale, severity]
   - Transformations:
     - Read YAML list from input/code_style.yaml.
     - Coerce id to string.
     - Normalize severity to UPPERCASE.
     - Sort entries by id ascending (lexicographic).

3) output/project/security-issues.json
   - Schema:
     - category: "security"
     - source_files: ["input/security_issues.csv"]
     - counts: { total: <int> }
     - entries: array with keys [id, title, severity, status, risk]
   - Transformations:
     - Read CSV from input/security_issues.csv (header present).
     - Deduplicate by id, keeping the last occurrence in the file.
     - Coerce id to string.
     - Normalize severity to UPPERCASE.
     - Compute numeric risk from severity using mapping: { "CRITICAL": 4, "HIGH": 3, "MEDIUM": 2, "LOW": 1 }. Any other value → 0.
     - Sort entries by id ascending (lexicographic).

4) output/project/ui-styles.json
   - Schema:
     - category: "ui_styles"
     - source_files: ["input/ui_styles.json"]
     - counts: { total: <int> }
     - entries: array with keys [type, name, value]
   - Transformations:
     - Read JSON object from input/ui_styles.json.
     - Flatten to tokens as follows:
       - For colors: create entries with type="color", name as the color key (e.g., "primary"), value as the hex string.
       - For typography: create entries with type="typography" for keys "font_family" and "base_size"; also expand the "scale" array into entries named "scale[0]", "scale[1]", ... with corresponding values.
     - Sort entries first by type ascending ("color" before "typography"), then by name ascending (lexicographic).

5) output/project/todolist.json
   - Schema:
     - category: "todos"
     - source_files: ["input/todos.tsv"]
     - counts: { total: <int> }
     - entries: array with keys [id, task, assignee, due_date, priority, status]
   - Transformations:
     - Read TSV from input/todos.tsv (header present, tab-delimited).
     - Deduplicate by id, keeping the last occurrence in the file.
     - Coerce id to string.
     - Normalize priority to UPPERCASE and status to lowercase.
     - Sort entries by id ascending (lexicographic).

General requirements for all trackers:
- Output must be valid JSON with the exact top-level keys: category, source_files, counts, entries.
- counts.total must equal the number of items in entries.
- Only write the five files listed above into output/project/.

Please proceed and write all five JSON trackers to their specified paths under output/project/.
